Title: The haplotype-resolved telomere-totelomere genome and OMICS analyses reveal genetic responses to tapping in rubber tree

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial elastomer; wound sealing/defense-related latex function
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High expression associated with enhanced rubber-producing capability and latex yield in cultivated rubber
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. REF1 is one of the most highly expressed REF/SRPP family genes in CATAS 7-33-97. 2. Its expression is significantly induced during key tapping-response stages. 3. The study links expansion/high expression of REF genes with high rubber-producing capability in cultivated rubber.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in latex, especially during key tapping-response stages
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in latex during tapping response
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomics; transcriptome profiling during tapping
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SRPP family proteins; CPT genes (contextual, pathway-associated)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate dosage target for improving rubber yield; may be useful in gene-stacking with other REF/SRPP members and MVA-pathway regulators
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, metabolomics, genome assembly; PRJCA022207; OMIX008934
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: REF/SRPP copy number expansion is associated with cultivated high-yield rubber; REF1 highlighted as the most strongly expressed member.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial elastomer; wound sealing/defense-related latex function
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Additional REF copy in cultivated rubber potentially contributes to high yield via gene dosage
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. REF2 is present in cultivated CATAS 7-33-97 but absent from the compared wild genome. 2. REF2 is tandemly arranged with REF3 and REF4. 3. Although lower than REF1, REF2 is still highly expressed in latex, supporting a dosage contribution to rubber yield.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in latex, though lower than REF1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful dosage-enhancement target in high-yield breeding
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of three tandemly arranged additional REF genes in cultivated rubber.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial elastomer; wound sealing/defense-related latex function
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Additional REF copy in cultivated rubber potentially contributes to high yield via gene dosage
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. REF3 is an extra cultivated-rubber REF gene relative to wild rubber. 2. REF3 is tandemly arranged with REF2 and REF4. 3. REF3 is highly expressed in latex despite lower transcript abundance than REF1.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in latex, lower than REF1
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted in discussion as a cultivated-rubber extra REF gene.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial elastomer; wound sealing/defense-related latex function
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Additional REF copy in cultivated rubber potentially contributes to high yield via gene dosage
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. REF4 is an extra REF gene in cultivated CATAS 7-33-97 compared with wild rubber. 2. REF4 is tandemly organized with REF2 and REF3. 3. REF4 shows high latex expression, supporting a possible dosage effect on yield.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in latex, lower than REF1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for yield-oriented germplasm selection
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included among additional cultivated REF copies proposed to contribute to high yield.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial elastomer; wound sealing/defense-related latex function
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High SRPP1 expression is associated with strong rubber-producing capability in cultivated rubber
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SRPP1 is among the most highly expressed REF/SRPP family genes in CATAS 7-33-97. 2. It shows significant induced expression during key tapping-response stages. 3. REF/SRPP family abundance and expression correlate with rubber-producing capability.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in latex, especially during key tapping-response stages
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in latex during tapping response
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative genomics; transcriptome profiling during tapping
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: REF family proteins; CPT genes (contextual, pathway-associated)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering target to improve rubber particle function and yield, potentially in combination with REF and MVK1/MYC2
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes REF1 and SRPP1 as the highest expressed members.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial elastomer; wound sealing/defense-related latex function
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Phosphorylates mevalonic acid to mevalonate-5-phosphate in the MVA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step during tapping-induced rubber biosynthesis in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes rubber biosynthesis and latex yield during tapping; strongly induced with increased MVA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MVK1 is the most significantly upregulated gene in the MVA pathway during consecutive tapping, with >10-fold transcript increase. 2. Its substrate MVA increases strongly during T7 and TN, and MVK1 expression shows an extremely significant correlation with MVA accumulation. 3. JA treatment upregulates MVK1 and increases rubber biosynthesis activity and yield. 4. HbMYC2 directly activates the MVK1 promoter, linking JA signaling to enhanced MVA flux and rubber production.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly induced in latex during consecutive tapping and after JA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly induced in latex during T1 to T7/TN transition and by exogenous JA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transcriptomics; metabolomics; correlation analysis; qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid; exogenous JA treatment; rubber synthesis activity and yield measurements
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: consecutive tapping/mechanical wounding; exogenous jasmonic acid treatment
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native HbMVK1 promoter; tested in reporter assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime target for improving rubber yield by increasing precursor flux; promising for promoter engineering or stacking with JA-response regulators such as MYC2
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MVK1 promoter contains a MYC-responsive element (G-box) absent in homologous MVK2 promoter according to the paper.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial elastomer; wound sealing/defense-related latex function
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Jasmonate metabolism linked to rubber biosynthesis regulation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: diverts flux toward target product via regulatory hormone activation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes methyl jasmonate into jasmonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes latex yield enhancement by increasing JA availability during tapping
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MJE1 was identified among latex-yield association candidate genes and showed 4.8-fold upregulation from T1 to T7. 2. The encoded enzyme hydrolyzes MeJA to JA. 3. Its induction further supports a crucial role of JA in enhancing latex yield during tapping.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated from T1 to T7 during tapping
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated during tapping response in latex
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: differential expression analysis linked with prior marker-trait association candidates
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream target to boost endogenous JA and rubber yield, though direct functional validation on yield remains limited in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned in Discussion as supportive evidence for JA-mediated yield enhancement rather than as the main experimentally validated target.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial elastomer; wound sealing/defense-related latex function
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: rubber biosynthesis precursor supply via MVA pathway activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates HbMVK1 transcription via promoter G-box binding
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Predicted hub JA-signaling TF and directly validated activator linking tapping-induced JA signaling to increased rubber biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with JAZ proteins in JA signaling pathway (context from paper discussion); direct complex not experimentally dissected here
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes rubber biosynthesis by activating MVK1 under tapping/JA response
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYC2 was predicted as the hub transcription factor in the regulatory network associated with tapping-induced rubber biosynthesis. 2. Endogenous JA increases during consecutive tapping, consistent with MYC2 activation in JA signaling. 3. Dual-luciferase assays showed HbMYC2 activates the HbMVK1 promoter by ~2.1-fold. 4. Y1H confirmed HbMYC2 binds the G-box cis-element in the HbMVK1 promoter. 5. The study proposes that JA-induced MYC2 enhances MVA-pathway flux and rubber production.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in latex during consecutive tapping-associated response network
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with tapping-induced transcriptional activation in latex
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: consecutive tapping/mechanical wounding; endogenous JA accumulation; exogenous JA treatment context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: STRING/PPI network inference; dual-luciferase reporter assay; yeast one-hybrid; transcriptome analysis; hormone profiling
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive effector for HbMYC2 in transient assay; native HbMVK1 promoter reporter
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate regulator for engineering tapping-responsive rubber yield; promoter tuning or JA-responsive circuit engineering may improve precursor flux
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central experimentally validated regulator in the paper’s proposed model.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial elastomer; wound sealing/defense-related latex function
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA signaling control of rubber biosynthesis response
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: JA-pathway transcriptional regulation via TF interaction/repression context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: works with/through MYC2-centered JA signaling network
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent JA-pathway regulator associated with elevated rubber synthetic activity during tapping
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of JA signaling homeostasis module
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Binds TFs such as MYC2 according to paper discussion
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with tapping-responsive JA signaling that promotes rubber biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAZ8 was identified among key TFs in the JA signaling-related regulatory network during tapping. 2. JAZ8 was significantly upregulated among amplified genes in cultivated rubber. 3. The study suggests JAZ8 may work with MYC2 to regulate increased rubber synthetic activity during tapping.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated during latex developmental/tapping stages in cultivated rubber
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transcriptome network analysis; comparative expansion analysis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning JA sensitivity, though direct causal validation on rubber yield is limited here
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as supportive regulator rather than direct target of mechanistic assays.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial elastomer; wound sealing/defense-related latex function
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: hormone-responsive transcriptional regulation associated with rubber biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: May act with MYC2 in tapping-responsive regulatory network
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent regulator associated with JA signaling and elevated rubber synthetic activity during tapping
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with increased rubber synthetic activity during tapping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ERF1B was identified as a key TF in the tapping-responsive network. 2. The paper suggests ERF1B may work together with MYC2 in JA signaling to regulate increased rubber synthetic activity. 3. Its overexpression pattern supports hormone crosstalk in yield regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in latex during tapping response
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: consecutive tapping/mechanical wounding; JA/ethylene signaling context
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible target for hormone-crosstalk engineering, but mechanistic validation remains limited in this study
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supportive regulator in network analysis; not directly validated as binding rubber biosynthesis gene promoters in this paper.
